perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or gross neglect of services that demonstrably harms the Company's financial standing by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the material breach of a core provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'Termination for Cause.' The original clause includes 'neglect of services' and 'breach of any provision' which is a broad criteria, while the changed clause specifies 'gross neglect of services that demonstrably harms the Company's financial standing' and 'material breach of a core provision.' This creates an **in-text contradiction** because if Section 8 refers to what Termination for Cause is, it now is less clearly defined, because the change is being more restrictive. The changed definition now contradicts any other use of the term 'Termination for Cause' because the criteria are substantially different.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (a) upon the death or bankruptcy of CONSULTANT; or (b) upon the close of business on the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of Termination for Cause (as defined below); (c) ninety (90) days following the date the Company gives CONSULTANT written notice of termination for any or no reason; or (d) CONSULTANT may terminate this Agreement by giving ninety (90) days' written notice to REIT. 4 For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or gross neglect of services that demonstrably harms the Company's financial standing by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the material breach of a core provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Termination for Cause' by adding requirements of 'gross neglect of services that demonstrably harms the Company's financial standing' and 'material breach of a core provision'. This contradicts the original broader definition in the contradicted text which allowed for termination based on 'any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services' and 'the breach or prospective breach of any provision'. By raising the threshold for what constitutes cause, the change creates an inconsistency as the contradicted text still refers to the original, broader definition.
